summary: GVE Level 2 defines the normative application-plugin composition layer for governed local CLI workflows. It preserves all accepted Level 0 effect, authority, evidence, fail-closed, and authoritative-result semantics and all accepted Level 1 workflow, operation, plugin-assignment, validated-operation-contract, dependency, handoff, lifecycle, and result boundaries. The Level 2 root establishes the authority, scope, document decomposition, and non-overlapping semantic responsibilities of the Level 2 specification set. Detailed composition semantics are owned by explicitly identified subordinate Level 2 documents. The root does not import subordinate documents, does not reinterpret plugin-specific meaning, and does not authorize maintained runtime implementation.

definitions.text: The application-independent normative layer that makes Level 1 workflow and application-plugin architecture precise enough for deterministic composition of governed local CLI operations while preserving all inherited authority, effect, evidence, failure, lifecycle, handoff, and result boundaries.

The complete normative Level 2 authority consisting of exactly one root document and the subordinate Level 2 documents identified by this root. Each document owns a distinct semantic responsibility and participates in the repository-wide deterministic specification graph.

The sole normative root document GVE-LEVEL-2. It establishes Level 2 identity, Level 1 parentage, inherited authority, composition-layer scope, subordinate-document decomposition, and exclusions. It names itself as the specification-set root and imports no subordinate document.
The subordinate Level 2 specification GVE-LEVEL-2-DOCUMENT-AUTHORITY, which owns normative import, visibility, identifier-resolution, parentage, and conflict semantics for the Level 2 document set.

The subordinate Level 2 specification GVE-LEVEL-2-WORKFLOW-COMPOSITION, which owns normative workflow membership, operation identity, selected-plugin binding, validated-operation-contract participation, ordering constraints, and workflow-plan acceptance semantics.

The subordinate Level 2 specification GVE-LEVEL-2-DEPENDENCIES-HANDOFFS, which owns normative dependency, sequencing, handoff declaration, actual-handoff validation, blocking, skipping, failure, and partial-progress semantics.

The subordinate Level 2 specification GVE-LEVEL-2-RESULT-ASSEMBLY, which owns normative operation-evidence aggregation and authoritative workflow-result assembly while preserving inherited effect-state distinctions.

The subordinate Level 2 specification GVE-LEVEL-2-LOCAL-PLUGIN-BOUNDARIES, which owns composition-facing responsibility boundaries for local filesystem modification, command execution, and local Git operation domains without defining concrete payload catalogs or maintained implementations.

The repository-root LEVEL_2.md design note retained as non-normative historical and discovery input. It has no authority over the normative Level 2 specification set and may not be incorporated without explicit review.

requirements.text: Level 2 must extend Level 1 and must not replace, weaken, collapse, contradict, or bypass any applicable Level 0 or Level 1 definition, requirement, authority boundary, effect distinction, fail-closed rule, evidence obligation, lifecycle boundary, dependency or handoff distinction, validated-operation-contract boundary, or limitation on authoritative result claims.

The Level 2 specification set must contain exactly one root document with identity GVE-LEVEL-2, numeric level 2, normative status, parent GVE-LEVEL-1, document role root, specification-set root GVE-LEVEL-2, and no imports.

The normative Level 2 subordinate-document set must consist of GVE-LEVEL-2-DOCUMENT-AUTHORITY, GVE-LEVEL-2-WORKFLOW-COMPOSITION, GVE-LEVEL-2-DEPENDENCIES-HANDOFFS, GVE-LEVEL-2-RESULT-ASSEMBLY, and GVE-LEVEL-2-LOCAL-PLUGIN-BOUNDARIES. Each subordinate document must own only the semantic responsibility assigned by this root.

The Level 2 root must not import subordinate documents, duplicate subordinate normative requirements, create precedence among subordinate documents, or make acceptance of the root stand for acceptance of any subordinate document.

Level 2 document responsibilities must be non-overlapping. A semantic question assigned to one subordinate document must not be redefined by another document; any apparent overlap, conflict, or unresolved ownership must fail closed until corrected through separately governed specification work.

The Level 2 composition layer must remain independent of plugin-specific operation meaning. The GVE core may consume only application-independent composition information established through inherited Level 1 boundaries and the accepted subordinate Level 2 specifications.

Identifiers declared by Level 2 normative documents must be stable, globally unique across the governed specification graph, and deterministically resolvable under the accepted document-authority rules. The Level 2 root reserves the prefix L2-ROOT for root requirement and relationship identifiers; subordinate documents must use distinct stable prefixes corresponding to their assigned responsibility.

Transitional Level 2 material is non-normative input only. It must not override accepted Levels 0 or 1, constrain the accepted decomposition, import GitHub or other remote-service plugin design into the initial Level 2 authority, or become normative by reference.

Acceptance of Level 2 specification authority does not authorize maintained GVE runtime behavior, concrete plugin payload schemas, exhaustive operation catalogs, production plugin implementations, GitHub or other remote-service plugin semantics, or Level 3 design.

includes: The sole normative Level 2 root identity, status, and Level 1 parentage
The Level 2 application-plugin composition-layer purpose and authority boundary
The final five-document subordinate decomposition by non-overlapping semantic responsibility
Normative versus non-normative Level 2 material status
Stable root and subordinate identifier namespace expectations
Core independence from plugin-specific operation meaning
Composition-facing local filesystem, command-execution, and local-Git domain boundaries delegated to a subordinate specification
excludes: Detailed document import, visibility, resolution, and conflict rules owned by GVE-LEVEL-2-DOCUMENT-AUTHORITY
Detailed workflow composition and plan-acceptance rules owned by GVE-LEVEL-2-WORKFLOW-COMPOSITION
Detailed dependency, sequencing, handoff, blocking, skipping, and partial-progress rules owned by GVE-LEVEL-2-DEPENDENCIES-HANDOFFS
Detailed evidence aggregation and authoritative workflow-result rules owned by GVE-LEVEL-2-RESULT-ASSEMBLY
Concrete local plugin payload schemas and operation catalogs
Maintained GVE runtime and plugin implementations
GitHub and other remote-service plugin semantics
Bootstrap executor mechanisms and SCF product authority
